marketState: neutral-bearish; extreme heat demand anchoring price near-term while storage surplus and seasonal headwinds build multi-week downside pressure
summary: Henry Hub front-month at $3.305 sits modestly above the fair-value midpoint of $2.960 but remains within the $2.460–$3.460 model band, so it is elevated but not yet stretched. The dominant near-term support is exceptional cooling demand: the 1–7 day and 8–16 day outlooks both breach the top of the 5-year historical range, with current demand running +45% above the 5-year normal — an extreme reading that historically keeps spot prices firm. Adding to the bullish short-term case, managed money is net short at only the 18th 5-year percentile, creating meaningful short-squeeze risk if the heat persists. However, the multi-week picture is unfavorable: seasonality gives only a 19% probability of a higher print in four weeks with a typical −1.8% move, storage stands +151 Bcf above the 5-year average (with a slightly bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise), and the weekly technical trend remains bearish (EMA 50<200, RSI 46.8). Futures curve data was unavailable due to API failures, though the mild front-over-next backwardation of $0.052 is consistent with near-term demand tightness.

longTerm.stop: $3.57/MMBtu (above the fair-value band ceiling of $3.46 with buffer; a close here invalidates the seasonal thesis)

longTerm.rationale: The 4-week seasonal probability distribution is decisively bearish — only 19% chance of a higher close, typical move −1.8% — and the +151 Bcf storage surplus above the 5-year average provides a structural drag that should weigh on prices as the injection season progresses. Price is above the fair-value midpoint now and remains above it across all 7/14/21-day fair-value projections, while the weekly technical structure (EMA 50<200, trend bearish) confirms the medium-term trend. Conviction is held at medium rather than high because extreme weather demand could delay the seasonal unwind and COT short-squeeze risk is real.
longTerm.invalidation: Weekly close above $3.57; sustained heat draws that materially reduce storage toward or below the 5-year average; managed-money net improves to above the 40th 5-year percentile (short covering rally taking hold)

shortTerm.rationale: Both the 1–7 day and 8–16 day demand forecasts exceed the 5-year historical maximum range, with demand running +45% above normal — the strongest near-term bullish factor in the dataset. The 4h technical structure is bullish (trend up, MACD positive, EMA 50>200, RSI 54), and managed money is at the 18th 5-year percentile net short, meaning a weather-driven squeeze could accelerate upside. keyDrivers: Weather demand 1–7D and 8–16D both above the 5-year historical maximum range, +45% vs 5-year normal — dominant near-term bullish catalyst
Managed-money net short at 18th 5-year percentile creates short-squeeze risk if extreme heat is sustained
Storage +151 Bcf above 5-year average with a slightly bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise — structural multi-week bearish drag

Fair value drifts higher ($2.96 → $3.18) over 21 days but price remains above the midpoint throughout, limiting fresh upside
risks: Rapid weather reversal: both 1–7D and 8–16D demand are at historical maximums and could deflate quickly on any forecast shift, removing the key near-term support
